raw: {"perspective": "Security", "findings": [{"severity": "high", "file_or_area": "atlas_swarm_daemon.py", "issue": "execute task type allows daemon to bypass swarm consultation, creating Class 3 solo-execution risk. No RLS enforced on task origin — could allow unprivileged executors to trigger privileged flows.", "recommended_fix": "Enforce RLS: only Security, Architecture, or CEO-originated tasks can schedule execute. Add swarm consultation gate even for 'safe' executors."}, {"severity": "medium", "file_or_area": "self-check loop", "issue": "Self-check loop may generate noise if stale state is frequent. No rate limit or backoff logic visible — risk of thrashing.", "recommended_fix": "Add exponential backoff and max retry limit to self-check tasks. Log decision rationale for audit."}, {"severity": "low", "file_or_area": "prod health curl removal", "issue": "Redundant monitoring removed, but no evidence of alerting handoff to GH Actions. Risk of alert fatigue or blind spots.", "recommended_fix": "Verify GH Actions alerts are wired to CEO/pager and logged in zeus.governance_events."}], "overall_verdict": "warn", "whistleblower_flag": null}

findings.issue: execute task type allows daemon to bypass swarm consultation, creating Class 3 solo-execution risk. No RLS enforced on task origin — could allow unprivileged executors to trigger privileged flows.
findings.recommended_fix: Enforce RLS: only Security, Architecture, or CEO-originated tasks can schedule execute. Add swarm consultation gate even for 'safe' executors.

findings.issue: Self-check loop may generate noise if stale state is frequent. No rate limit or backoff logic visible — risk of thrashing.
findings.recommended_fix: Add exponential backoff and max retry limit to self-check tasks. Log decision rationale for audit.

findings.issue: Redundant monitoring removed, but no evidence of alerting handoff to GH Actions. Risk of alert fatigue or blind spots.
findings.recommended_fix: Verify GH Actions alerts are wired to CEO/pager and logged in zeus.governance_events.
